reviews.text: Was disappointed with this hotel. When I checked into the hotel, the staff told us they had missed out on our booking ; all they asked is for us to wait and not committing to us how long we have to wait . In additional, they prepared another room where apparently there was some renovation on the Neighbouring room which was relatively quite noisy . The scenery of the hotel was bad as you can see the poorly maintenance of their greenery at the hotel premise when you walk to the swimming pool . Smelly room and toilet .

reviews.title: Very unpleasant stay
reviews.positives: The only thing I like about this property is the garden in the back with the pool.
reviews.negatives:
No AC in lobby, corridor, restaurant.
No lights after dark in the corridor, it looks like one of those haunted movie when it’s dark.
The beautiful garden has no light after 6PM when this is the most beautiful area of this hotel and it could single-handedly literally lights up the entire hotel and makes the hotel super magical.
Can’t believe a 4 stars hotel turns off AC for the whole building 24/24 to save for electricity. I paid almost 200$ for 2 nights and never seen anything like this.
The staffs always talk about their dramas in the hotel loudly, this is so unprofessional for a 4 stars hotel. 
The beach is also very dirty with trash everywhere.

reviews.title: Bad experience
reviews.text:
Staffs are  unfriendly,  floor in the elevator is very dirty with a lot of water and dust from people back from sea and pool.
Our room was very noisy, lots of noise and we can’ even rest. 
Breakfast buffets were another disappointed, menu is repeated and is restricted with little choices. 
Never come back there again

reviews.title: Very good

reviews.negatives: When there were lots of guests, it was very loud at night. Otherwise a good place to stay at

reviews.title: Poor customer service and horrible housekeeping
reviews.text:
No bellboy was available to assist us with the luggage when we check in. Customer service was poor. The room housekeeping was horrible. The floor was not properly vacuum, hair everywhere. Bedsheet was not fitted nicely. 

The common balcony idea was sucks. No privacy at all. Can see underwear hanging around the common balcony.

Experienced blackout twice in a day. No air con and water heater during the blackout period. Tap water was yellowish.

Breakfast was bad. Nothing is delicious. It is a two stars hotel standard. It is far from a four stars hotel standard. Will never want to stay again. Very disappointed with Muong Thanh.

reviews.title: Khách sạn tệ nhất từ trước tới giờ (The worst hotel ever)
reviews.text:
(*English below)
------------------
Nhân viên đứng chào khách bất lịch sự. Khách ngồi sofa chờ checkin, vừa đứng lên thì nhân viên lấy chổi phủi phủi. 
Phòng nghỉ rất dơ và hôi. Tinh trùng bắn khắp nơi nhưng ko ai dọn dẹp.
Giường gối hôi mốc. 
Khuôn viên ngoại cảnh xấu, không ai chăm chúc. Sảnh hành lang vô phòng bụi và rác. 
Giá khách sạn 4 sao nhưng chất lượng dịch vụ và cơ sở hạn tầng như nhà nghỉ.
---------------------------------
Staff greets guests impolite.  Guests sat on the sofa waiting for checkin, just stood up, the staff took a broom to brush it off.
 Room was very dirty and smelly.  Sperm splashed everywhere but no one cleaned up.
 Musty pillow bed.
 The campus outside is bad, no one is attentive.  Hallway to the dust and garbage room.
The price of a 4-star hotel, but the quality of service and infrastructure is like a motel.
reviews.response:
Kính gửi Anh/ Chị Thinh Uyen,

Cám ơn Quý khách và gia đình đã chọn Khách sạn Mường Thanh Mũi Né cho kỳ nghỉ của mình và có những ý kiến chia sẻ, đóng góp về khách sạn.

Trước hết, chúng tôi chân thành xin lỗi về những vấn đề mà Quý khách đã gặp phải trong thời gian Quý khách và gia đình lưu trú tại khách sạn. Đây thật sự không phải là trải nghiệm mà chúng tôi muốn Quý khách và gia đình của mình có tại khách sạn của chúng tôi.

Chúng tôi muốn Quý khách và gia đình biết rằng chúng tôi rất coi trọng mối quan tâm của Quý khách cũng như gia đình và chúng tôi sẽ chia sẻ điều này với tất cả nhân viên của chúng tôi. Chúng tôi sẽ nỗ lực hơn nữa để đảm bảo rằng tất cả khách của chúng tôi đều có một kỳ nghỉ thoải mái.

Cảm ơn Quý khách rất nhiều vì đã cho chúng tôi cơ hội nghe ý kiến của Quý khách và cải thiện dịch vụ của chúng tôi. 

Xin chân thành cảm ơn.
Mr. Hoàng Hùng - Giám Đốc Khách Sạn

reviews.title: Don't stay here unless you like loud speakers
reviews.text:
Booked here for a night and within one hour was asking for a refund. They host company bonding days here which if anyone is familiar with Vietnam means loud speakers on the beach. The hotel looks pieceful and relaxing but it is the exact opposite. I was told by staff that they regularly host staff corporate days. I wouldn't stay here again if I was offered a room for free.

In addition to the risk of being subject to loud speakers all day. The hotel looks worn and is a long way from the main area. The whole hotels smells of cigarettes and lemongrass. Given the number of other excellent properties in Mui Ne (I've been here several times as I live in Vietnam), I'd never stay here again.

The member of staff was sympathetic to our situation. Im guessing Im not the first person to complain but it appears the owners don't care about their guests, just corporate money. They offered us some free fruit. We declined.
reviews.response:
Dear Mr Joel,
 
Greetings from Mường Thanh Holiday Mui Ne Hotel!

We're so sorry your problems. We would also like to thank and receive valuable information from you.

We hope you will continue to return to our hotel in the future to have a better chance to serve you.

We wish you a great day!

Kind regards, 
Ms Mỹ Thể - Mường Thanh Holiday Muine Hotel

reviews.text:
All the negative commends below had been validated and proven 
1. AC is too small to cold the room and it took us few hours to make the room comfortable. 
2. The spa is strongly not recommended, there are few available across the street. 
Tipping should depend on the customer satisfaction and tip accordingly. The masseuses're too greedy to request at least 30% of the total cost when we wanted to tip. 
3. Be aware and be warned ! Especially for those who planned to stay few nights. There are 2 compliment waters for guest (outside the fridge) and 2 in the fridge must pay during checkout. Dont be a smart axx by changing those inside fridge with outside. On our 1st night, both are 350ml (no harm to change). However, my partner was unaware that fridge has placed with 500ml on the 2nd night.

Chúng tôi gửi lời xin lỗi chân thành đến Quý khách vì những vấn đề không hay đã xảy ra trong thời gian Quý khách lưu trú nghỉ dưỡng tại Khách sạn Mường Thanh chúng tôi.
	
Chúng tôi cũng xin chân thành cảm ơn và tiếp thu những thông tin đóng góp ý kiến quý báu của Quý khách. Chúng tôi cam kết sẽ nỗ lực hết mình để Khách sạn có cơ hội được hoàn thiện, nâng cao và đáp ứng chất lượng sản phẩm và dịch vụ tốt hơn.

\Một lần nữa chúng tôi thành thật xin lỗi sự phiền lòng của Quý khách trong thời gian vừa qua. Chúng tôi hy vọng nhận được sự cảm thông của Quý khách và mong muốn sẽ tiếp tục được phục vụ Quý khách trong thòi gian tới.

Xin cảm ơn và chúc Quý khách có mọi điều tốt đẹp. 

Trân trọng,
Nguyễn Anh Dũng - Giám Đốc Khách Sạn

reviews.negatives:
Sauf heureuses exceptions, le personnel ne parle pas anglais, donc difficultés et qui pro quo, style: « NE CHANGEZ PAS NOS SERVETTES ET PEIGNOIRS, on ne reste que 3 nuits. RÉP: D’accord!’Resultat: tout changé, chaque matin 🤪. Bonjour l’écologie ! Par contre, la clim s’est éteinte ( pour éviter les gaspillages) au beau milieu de la nuit, me réveillant par la chaleur : j’ai dû prévenir la réception pour déprogrammer cette option, car j’ai du mal à me rendormir….surtout si les établissements voisins émettent de la musique assourdissante jusqu’aux aurores!
En tout cas, le staff est très poli et à l’écoute. Seul bémol : il faudrait qqun qui nettoie la plage de l’hôtel des ordures plastiques qui défigurent ce coin de paradis. Personnellement, je l’ai fait un peu  tous les jours : il faudrait une politique sérieuse de récolte et tri des ordures, une réduction du plastique, une SENSIBILISATION de tout le monde aux problèmes environnementaux. L’océan Pacifique est merveilleux, il faut arrêter de le traiter en poubelle 🥺

reviews.title: Schöne Ablage zum entspannen.
reviews.positives: Die Außenanlagen ist sehr schön und gepflegt, man kann direkt am Strand liegen, jedoch kein Sandstrand sondern so steinstufen. Mit dem Roller ist man in 20 Minuten in Phan Thiet und noch kürzer in Mui Ne. Die Zimmer sind ganz nett, jedoch ein kleiner Balkon ohne balkonmöbel. Es gibt ein Gym, Tischtennis und Billard, das war super. Das Frühstück war auch gut! Der Restaurant-Service und Pool-Service war sehr unpersönlich, kein Englisch, kein Lächeln. Der Service an der Rezeption war freundlich. Wir hatten unsere Wäsche abgegeben und wurden dann mit einer 1.000.000 Euro Rechnung überrascht. Grund war, dass jedes Teil einzelne berechnet wurde, dies wurde uns so nicht erklärt, vermutlich auch wegen der Sprachbarriere. Also haben wir knapp 40 Euro für einen Sack Wäsche bezahlt. Normalerweise gibt man in Vietnam einen Sack ab, der dann ca 200.000 kostet oder ein Kilo 50.000. Das Personal kann nichts für die Preise, aber sollte deutlicher darauf hinweisen. Hier ist Vorsicht geboten.

Kính gửi chị Thanh Thảo,

Cám ơn chị và gia đình đã chọn Khách sạn Mường Thanh Mũi Né cho kỳ nghỉ của mình và có những ý kiến chia sẻ, đóng góp về khách sạn.

Trước hết, chúng tôi chân thành xin lỗi về những vấn đề mà chị đã gặp phải trong thời gian chị lưu trú tại khách sạn. Đây thật sự không phải là trải nghiệm mà chúng tôi muốn chị và gia đình của mình có tại khách sạn của chúng tôi.

Es gibt viel was man machen kann, Billard, Tischtennis, Fitness usw… der Pool ist super sauber und schön. Ausnahmslos jeder im Hotel war sehr freundlich. Das Frühstück war abwechslungsreich. reviews.negatives: Die Einrichtung in den Zimmern ist in die Jahre gekommen. Die Pool Security sollte den Schlüssel für Getränke haben. Der Liegenbereich war im Verhältnis zu anderen Hotels in Mui Ne sauber, dennoch sollte dort der Müll im Sand den zum Teil die Gäste hinterlassen entfernt werden.

reviews.title: worst hotel in Muine ever!
reviews.text: I can feel that it was recently opened hotel because everything was not organized. Check in has been done really late while there was not so much guests. Room was quite spacious though.. Nothing to eat for breakfast n specially the water in the bathroom was soo~~ weak and the color was nearly red which feels like unproperly purified. Garden n swimming pool was quite good n beautiful.. for once experience in cheap price was fair enough but I will never return nor recommand to other people!!
reviews.positives: large swimming pool

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: Personale gentilissimo e disponibile. Piscina grande e comoda che affaccia sul mare; non tutte le strutture da quelle parti offrono questo privilegio. Camere spaziose e pulite. Nonostante sia ad un km dal vero centro di Mui né vicino ci sono molti ristoranti e ogni tipo di servizio, anche lavanderia. Lo staff è davvero gentile e vi aiuterà anche nella prenotazione di biglietti per gli spostamenti.

reviews.title: Won’t Return
reviews.text: Can’t say I was impressed by this property at all. The pool was the only good thing. You couldn’t walk on the beach because the water was choppy and the waves were crashing against the boundary wall, the gardens looked unkept. The balconies had no furniture. The bathrooms had a bathtub shower combination only suitable for people 6feet snd above who can climb in and out without nearly slipping over.  I went to the spa  seeking a treatment and was greeted by an old man with a cigarette in his mouth wondering why I was there!!  Thr pool  bar rarely had someone available for service and the beds by the pool were not equipped with covers to lay on. Oh dear, not a great experience at all. The staff were lovely but then again Vietnamese people are lovely in general. I paid for the full 12 days but ended up leaving after 3 days snd stayed at a better hotel in a better location and enjoyed my holiday.

reviews.title: Not so Disappointing Stay, yet Poor Room Facilities and Unimpressive Service
reviews.text:
I recently stayed at Muong Thanh Mui Ne, and while it served as a place to sleep, I found the overall experience quite lacking. The room facilities were disappointing: the lighting was dim, Wi-Fi speed was frustratingly slow, and the TV was tiny and not a smart TV, which felt quite outdated. 

Breakfast was included, but I was let down by the limited options. The hot dishes weren’t kept warm, and the food was served cold, which impacted the experience. At the soup station, the cook seemed irritated when I asked for assistance and even instructed me to keep an eye on the eggs while they were cooking—which I thought was her responsibility, not mine. 

Overall, Muong Thanh Mui Ne felt like little more than a place to sleep. I expected more from my stay, and unfortunately, it didn’t meet those expectations.

reviews.title: OK resort - I expect more
reviews.text: After looking up for a place to stay just after Tet holiday, I found Muong Thanh to be a bright candidate for our family with our little boy. I held high expectation for this resort given quite a popular name in Vietnam's hotel chain and the nice pictures on Agoda. The biggest plus of the trip is the swimming pool, both I and my son enjoy it which is very nice. The let down is the room which is quite old, although it is quite spacious the bed is not most comfortable once I tried in Mui Ne. We arrive at 1 PM and I told the check-in staff if they can arrange a room for us since my son fallen as sleep and we would like to check him in the room so he can sleep better but we were told there is no room available and we had to wait until 2 PM.  The food is ok with Western and Eastern cuisines. The beach is not that clean so we end up spend most of our time in the pool.

reviews.title: Bad service
reviews.text: The hotel was alright. The staff or hotel policy is bad. We reached at 3am on night bus hoping to check in, get some sleep n travel in the morning... the staff don’t let us check in despite we try option like paying more for upgrades room or etc. The staff was not around at 1st too, no one was around until we find out he was sleeping on the sofa. His mentality was not letting us checked in until 2pm. And ask us to go and sleep in order hotel n back again to check in at 2pm. Even though more expensive room is not full. We wait until 6am and ask him again, well, now he say can check in but have to oay 50% extra charged... I was like, why u let us waited 3hours and now say ok we can pay to check in... there is no point if we need to wait n pay more for that extra rest, not worth it and so we leave our bag there and went outside. Also room will not be clean automatically, need to ask! Bad management!

reviews.title: Terrible shower and tub
reviews.text:
The tub is over three feet high, almost needing a stepladder to get up and into it.  The tub has no rough surface and is very slippery to stand in.  The shower nozzle is not adjustable and there is only a short glass partition. 
So the shower sprays water over the floor, making getting into and out of the tub even more slippery.  There are no handles or railings to hold on to.  It is unclean and dangerous.  My wife and I slipped numerous times and almost fell.  Very poor bathroom design.

reviews.title: Old hotel, sparse grounds.
reviews.text:
- The NDC road section is under repair (it's taking a long time and has been almost 2 years) so the road is muddy and cluttered. It's dangerous to walk at night.
- I booked a Deluxe King room but when I checked in, it was a twin room with two single beds.
- The room light flickers and even after notifying staff to replace it, it is still unstable.
- The balcony door lock is stuck tight, and the bathroom door lock is also faulty with no one to replace them.
- The internet is intermittent, sometimes working and sometimes not, and I had to rely on a 4G Wi-Fi hotspot; the TV is old, very small (24 inches), not smart, and the picture quality is fuzzy, watching TV feels like the 90s...
- The balconies of the adjacent rooms do not ensure privacy or security at all.
- The breakfast is sparse with just a few items (white rice with braised fish, sweet potatoes, fried instant noodles, sausages...); the only Western options are cold cuts, butter, and bread, and very poor except for the soup phở and eggs, which are good.
- The staff's friendly and attentive attitude in carrying luggage and directing parking is a plus.
This hotel does not deserve a 4-star rating; I believe it only merits around 3 stars at best, while the room prices are those of a 4-star hotel.

reviews.title: Simple fixes would make this great
reviews.text:
We mostly enjoyed our stay but there were a few little fixes that just made me feel like they didn’t care or wouldn’t fix issues.

The gym without aircon or water on a 34° is not great (or safe). The aircon is broken and they haven’t fixed it.

The cashier was away the whole time so we couldn’t exchange money and you have to grab to 3km away to get to atms. Not terrible just not ideal.

We had issues ordering room service. It ended up being quite good but by the second order there was so much confusion someone came up to our room and realised we had an old room service menu with so much nice food on it. They took that menu and gave us a much smaller one with nothing that we wanted. They had made our order previously which wasn’t even on the new menu 😂 and charged us incorrectly for something else. I thought it was the language barrier but it was because the menu was wrong so they just made something else.

I also had a dish at the restaurant. Onion and celery beef. I wasn’t sure if I got the right order since there was no celery and it was too spicy to eat (no chilli mentioned).

They saw me struggling and took it away. Brought it back with some of the chilli gone but the rest of the dish was just as hot. She added rice and said it was for free which was nice but I could only eat the rice. Up at the counter they charged us for the meal anyway.

The room was nice.

reviews.title: The price is high and not suitable for children.
reviews.text:
I booked through Agoda and got a 10% discount.  
Room type: Deluxe King Room with a sea view. The room has a bathtub.  
Price: 1,697,000 VND/night on Friday evening.  
The welcome drink is fresh watermelon juice.  
My room has a direct view of the sea and the pool, so it looks quite chill.

Nearby, there is Ham Tien market and many stores and eateries, making it easy to walk around for meals and buy essentials. I bought bottled water opposite for 13k and avocado smoothie for 20k, which is a reasonable price without being overpriced. Renting a motorbike costs 100k, which is cheap. Breakfast is delicious, the space is open and airy, and food is continuously replenished despite the large number of guests during my visit. So I feel that Muong Thanh is just a place to eat and sleep.

Now for the downsides:  
The room is deteriorating, and the door is broken, which is not consistent with the price.  
I think this place is not suitable for children as there is no indoor play area and the outdoor play area is very dirty and rundown. The space is also small, and the surrounding greenery is untidy and not well-maintained.

The beach is dirty, the sandy area is quite bad and smelly, so you can't swim or even take a walk – that's a criticism!  
The check-in area is also unattractive and lacks air conditioning. There are only a few lights on, making it quite dim.

Since there is no play area, the child played in the room watching the sea from afar.  
I think the following groups of guests would suit Muong Thanh:  
- Tour groups that need a resting place and good breakfast before continuing their day.  
- Couples visiting Mui Ne for the first time who want a nice place to sleep and eat, swim in the pool and then leave.  
For me, it's not suitable because I want to stay in the hotel and enjoy the amenities all day.

I rate Muong Thanh 5 out of 10 and have no intention of returning.

The breakfast  on sunday morning, the only "crowded" day of the week was a mess, most of the dishes were finished before the close time and it were not refilled, and in the Monday morning with almost no customer the variety was very limited.  
Then in the Swimming pool, the highlight of the hotel, they ran out of towel both days I visited, but also both days it was no even customers to made it possible... Staff in the pool was not interesting in provide any help. 
I must say that the few reception staff were the only motivated to offer a pleasent stay. 
Hope this situation is only because of the current situation and it is not always like this.

reviews.title: It felt like the cost performance was not very good.
reviews.text:
From the moment we entered the accommodation, there was a musty smell that required ventilation, and the sink wasn't draining properly, with the water-blocking button already broken and detached. We booked three places, and in one of them, as soon as we closed the bathroom door, a large tile fell from the ceiling, which could have seriously injured someone if they had been inside. In another room, the table broke when we placed some items on it... 

The swimming pool was filled with water, but it was cold with the wind blowing, and they were short on towels, which we couldn’t take to our room, so we had to dry ourselves at the pool and return the towels immediately. The kids were all cold as they moved back to the accommodation...

There wasn't much to eat for breakfast, and because it was known to be a popular spot for Koreans, there was no kimchi available... Haha. 

The only good thing was that there was a ping pong table, and we briefly played ping pong? Haha. 

The location isn’t bad, but the beach at Jangyong Beach had almost no sandy area, and the waves crashed right as you went down the stairs, so we couldn't even think of using the beach. Overall, it was too outdated for a family trip with kids.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text:
I would like to provide some feedback based on my stay at Mường Thanh Mũi Né, hoping the hotel can improve and enhance the quality of service:

Regarding the lobby area: It is unclear why the air conditioning in the lobby has not been turned on, making it quite hot, especially when guests are waiting to check-in/check-out, which can create a feeling of discomfort and stuffiness. With a 4-star hotel standard, I believe that maintaining air conditioning in the lobby would greatly improve the customer experience.

Regarding the swimming pool: The pool is large, clean, and quite beautiful. However, there is currently no area or foot wash slab before entering the pool, which allows sand from the beach or dirt from outside to easily enter the pool, somewhat affecting general cleanliness.

Regarding the guest rooms: The rooms are spacious and airy; however, the air conditioning is not cool enough for the room size. Additionally, the bathroom does not have an exhaust fan, which can make it feel a bit stuffy after use. The common hallway outside the room is also quite hot and stuffy due to the lack of air conditioning, especially when just stepping out of the elevator.

Regarding the beach: The hotel's beach area is quite difficult to swim in due to the deep water and large waves.

On a positive note, the reception staff and other employees are very friendly, supportive, and eager to assist.

I provide this feedback entirely in a constructive spirit and hope the hotel will consider further improvements to provide a better experience for customers in the future. Thank you to the hotel.

The beach is quite dirty, which is a common situation in this area. The sea wall makes the sand not look good. 
The staff is excellent, very helpful and polite.

reviews.title: Not bad not good
reviews.text: 01st thing our group came across at the hotel is the horrible noise from the party by pool side of the hotel. They allowed customer to sing karaoke with speakers facing towards the guests rooms. When we ask until what time the party will last, they told us to be before 10PM (which is very late). Phan Thiet is a destination that families usually choose for weekend vacation. I dont know why the hotel allow noisy parties like this to happen during weekend nights and last till very late .  2nd thing is that Muong Thanh seems to do not care about their beach front view, trash and wastewater and the smell are all over the near by area and noone actually dare to swim in the sea. If they are making money on the land, I think they should take care of it themselves.

reviews.title: This place could be so much better!
reviews.text: We stayed here 5 nights and although there was nothing bad about this place just the general upkeep was slacking. The housekeeping appeared to work all day and hard but didn’t get to our room until evening, maybe they were understaffed. Kitchen staff were good. One guy at the pool was really helpful while another just sat on his phone so we sat on a subbed without the cover! You can put everything on your room, we were a bit disappointed you had to pay to play pool. Then finally, we settled our bill the last evening but then they came running after us in the morning for a water they had forgot to charge us for, seemed a bit excessive.

reviews.text:
Although the hotel was big and we got upgraded to a 2 bedder room. There was a huge space between the bed and the TV making it rather hard to enjoy the channels provided. The room was also not very clean. A better job could be done of the place. As it was easy to find dust and hair on the floor.
The facilities while appearing to be plenty weren't that great after all. It was just a hotel with a pool, a small gym that isn't air conditioned as well as massage parlour in the basement. One thing that stood out to me was that it wasn't easy for us navigate through the premises. Each of the facilities were spaced far apart with not much navigational signs leading us the way. And to get to the pool from our room it took about 6 minutes from our door to the water. On top of that we had to cross a garden while the water sprinklers were on.

The hotel has room which can be occupied by 3 people and you can pay with the price for 2 people. My group consist of 14 people and several of my team member got this kind of room. 

We tried to eat at the seafood restaurant next to Muong Thanh Mui Ne Hotel on the day when we plan to leave Mui Ne to Dalat. It was a good restaurant .

reviews.title: worth for money
reviews.text:
We chose this hotel because of its good price with breakfast, but after we arrived there we found it's a bit far away from the main attraction area, like boke street, joe's bar. Considering the go-and-return taxi fee every time you want to get there, maybe it's equivalent to choose some other hotels near the boke street area, just few minutes you can walk to there. Anyways, this hotel is ok with budget money. And the swimming pool is pretty nice, facing to the beach. The breakfast is fine. However, we found some white color things in the sofa of our room at the first day, we don't know what's that (maybe other's sperm, disgusting). And the wifi signal in the 3rd floor is terrible. And the LAN cable port doesn't work.



At last, one small suggestion to all travellers visiting Vietnam: Please try not to book the "Hanh Cafe" bus tickets to Mui Ne or return. It's a nightmare for us to take their bus from Mui Ne to Ho Chi Mingh City. They promised us to pick up us at hotel at 7.20am but we wait till 7.50am. After we got on the bus, their two staffs forced us to change our original seats to some broken seats and leave our seats to their Vietnam people. We are not willing to change at the beginning, however, they scolded us loudly with extremely bad manner, threw our belongings and even want to beat us. That's really a crazy behavior. We even didn't figure our why they would treat their customers in this way. Maybe that's their way, they did the same thing to the other English couple. We tried to argue with them but finally we found it doesn't work, and we just kept calm, as long as they can take us to Ho Chi Mingh safely. Maybe we are just the unfortunate guys, but please do not repeat our experience to try to take Hanh Cafe bus, even their price is cheaper than others'.
reviews.positives: worth for money, swimming pool, friendly staff

reviews.title: Very nice room
reviews.text:
- The room were not available at the time I checked in @ 15h00 24/02/2015.
The reception asked me to wait for 20 minutes. Then when I asked again at 15h40, the room were not ready also. They asked me to keep waiting for the next 20 more minutes.

When I booked on Agoda, I left the note with my phone number. So in case the hotel might have any problems with the room availability, they can call me to inform me in advance. 

I were not feeling comfortable at being waited for an hour in the lobby. Especially I had spent 4 and a half hour driving from Ho Chi Minh City. 

- I spent 3 days at this hotel. And the Internet connection speed was slow on the first 2 days I stayed. 

- I called the reception/ lobby/hotel restaurant to order the food for in-room service. But no one picked up the phone. I called at 10Pm while the in-room service time is from 6Am-23Pm. 
( There is no restaurant near by walking )

- The beach is not good to play/swim here. Because there are all rocks at the bottom. 

Finally I don't think I would come back to this again. Although the hotel decoration & rooms are very nice. 

Thanks all
reviews.positives: Very nice room, Quite area & Good foods

reviews.title: Normal
reviews.text: The hotel, quite old and situated far from the city center, has a road covered in dust. The actual experience doesn’t quite match the pictures. However, the hotel does have a large pool.
reviews.response:
Dear Linh,

Thank you for choosing to stay with us and for sharing your feedback.

We appreciate your comments regarding of your stay.

At the same time, we sincerely apologize that some aspects of your experience did not meet your expectations, particularly. Your feedback is very valuable to us, and we will continue to review and improve our facilities and services.

We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back and provide you with a better experience in the future.

Best regards,
Phạm Tấn Oai 
General Manager
